---
title: 'readme'
format: html
jupyter: python3
python:
  path: .venv/bin/python
execute:
  dir: project
---

### Create a project Vitual Environment

(highly recommended, but not required)

` ` ` run in terminal

python3 -m venv venv

Code chunks must be run in order. If you make changes to inputs in an earlier code chunk rerun all (and review manual overrides as needed) chunks below in order.

` ` ` ` ` `  run in terminal if venv not activated in new session

source venv/bin/activate

## Install Required Packages

(install scn_redcap_clean in editable mode)

` ` ` {run in terminal}

pip install -e .

` ` ` ` ` `  {run in terminal if the code chunk in your qmd aren't running}

pip install jupyter

# Imports and Config
For additional config settings you can also look in config.py

(If you change anything in this chunk, or any config.py variables you must rerun all the
chunks below in order)

```{python}
#| label: Imports and Config
#%reload_ext autoreload # comment out if you're not editting py files

from scn_redcap_clean import Cleaner, config # local import


# Data Dictionary
config.data_dict = 'ToyDict.csv'

# Module Specific
config.modules = ['toy',] # as listed in the Data Dictionary 'Form Name' column

# csv must have all columns of the module(s) you are filtering by (extra modules/cols are fine)
config.raw_module_csv = 'Toy1'

config.c_genomic = 'genomic_text1'
config.p_genomic = 'genomic_p._text2'
# all columns marked text in the data dictionary you don't want translated
config.no_translate_cols = [
      config.c_genomic,
      config.p_genomic,
      config.birthdate,
      config.merge_on_id_column,
      ]

# alternatively you can manual set the text_columns you wanted translated below

```

**Set Files to Merge and Columns to Translate**
Cleaner() will auto create a 'raw' folder for you to add your raw data files and a
'ref' folder for you to add your Data Dictionary
(it will also auto create additional project folders in your data directory)

(see Extras below for using a different raw folder name)
```{python}
#| label: Cleaner instance

# see Extras below for using a different raw folder name

cleaner = Cleaner()

```

##### Extra Functions/Configs:

Custom raw data folder name
if you want your raw data folder stored separately from this .qmd file this should be the entire folder path

config.raw_data_dir = 'raw_data'    # run before running cleaner = Cleaner()
cleaner = Cleaner()

` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` `




## 01 Create merge_df with age added and output translations


**Make sure raw data is in the raw folder & data dictionary in the ref folder before continuing**
Also ensure your data dictionary and modules are accurate (config. in the first code chunk)


Outputs 01_merged_raw to the steps folder, translations_manual_override for review and
edits in the review folder, and a 01_merged_raw.txt for logging any manual override
explanations to the overrides folder.

Archive also stores a read only copy of 01_merged_raw.csv and
translations_manual_override.csv which will be used for auto summaries.
Rerunning the same stage with different inputs will overwrite files in the 'steps' folder.
Version history csv files are stored in the 'archive' folder for every run with different
back to back input if the contents of 01_merged_raw is changed.
(e.g. 2 runs in a row without *any* changes will not create new archive files)

The data dictionary is used as a map to automatically get all 'Field Type' = 'text' for
translation.
config.no_translate_cols = [] above should include all columns you don't want translated.

See Extra for manually setting specific columns you do want translated instead.
```{python}
#| label: 01 Merge Raw and Review Translations

csv_list = ['Toy1', 'Toy2.csv']  # raw data csvs for merge

# if specific terms need overrides after translation add them here & rerun this step
config.translation_dict = { # planning to track this in notes eventually for each version
            '布洛芬':'Ibuprofen',
           }

cleaner.step_01_merge_raw_and_review_translations(csv_list)
```

Other Info:
- Any exact duplicate columns in more than one csv will only be included once.
- A suffix '_{filename}' will be added to identical column names containing at least one
different input for a given participant id.
- config.translation_dict input:
non-english must be the key and the translation (or medical term) the value


##### Extra Functions/Configs:

*Manually setting text columns for translation*
List all langauge-dependent text columns (exclude genomic text, etc.) for translation
e.g.
text_columns = ['toy_text1', 'toy_text2', 'toy_text3', 'toy_text4', 'toy_text5']
cleaner.step_01_merge_raw_and_review_translations(csv_list, text_columns)

If translations are failing involving non-latin scripts adjust config.translation_script_threshold
(detects non-latin characters and assigns language after 3 characters match a specific char. script)
run anywhere after cleaner = Cleaner() and before get_01_merge_raw_and_review_translations()

e.g.
config.translation_script_threshold = 4    # if you don't have many text_columns this should be 1 or 2


By default filter_columns = 'birthdate' for creating the duplicate review csv but you
should be able to use more than 1 column;
avoid any open text responses for filters due to typos

e.g.
filter_columns = ['birthdate', 'country']
cleaner.get_01_merge_raw_and_review_translations(csv_list, text_columns, filter_columns = filter_columns)

` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` ` `



## 02 Overwrite non-english with english translations and output duplicates for review


After outputing translations, manually review and edit translations_manual_override.csv
in the review folder as needed. A version history of the translations file is saved in
archive for record keeping. The final column in translations_manual_override should be
used to record reasons you made any adjustments to a row.
**Once manual edits are complete move this file to the overrides folder.**

Keep the final version of this file without altering filename or column names in the
manual_overrides folder *before running* get_02_translated_and_review_duplicates()

The .txt file in the overrides folder is for recording general and brief override
explanations and row deletion (e.g. 'Deleted false positives, language was already english.')
Keep this file in the overrides folder (the summary will eventually archive it).



02_translated.csv is saved to the steps folder. Duplicates are identified by birthdate
and duplicates_manual_override.csv is saved to review.

```{python}
#| label: 02 Translated and Review Duplicates

cleaner.step_02_translated_and_review_duplicates()

```




## 03 Add duplicate manual overrides, remove duplicates, and output medications for review


Again, manually review and edit duplicates_manual_override.csv as needed, leaving
explanations in the overrides column of the manual_overrides file and general comments
in the txt.


*For manual overrides Participant_id should be written as {last Participant_id per bday}.999*
Example:
participant_id's for birthdate 1/2/02 are 3, 5, 8
If a manual override is needed the override should be 8.999

(.888 works too, just choose an integer that stands out for you.) If no manual override
(last submission is best) these rows do not need to be included in the manual override,
but it should still work if they are. Add the final version without altering column
names to the overrides folder.

If **all** duplicates do not need a *manual* override, you do not need to move the
duplicate overrides file to the overrides folder, just run the code chunk below to
remove earlier submission duplicates.

Duplicates are identified based on birthdate. (Confirm they are actual duplicates and
not just **shared** birthdates.)

**keep flag_shared_birthdate empty, unless two different participants truly share a birthdate**

See 'Shared Birthdates' below for instructions if needed.

**Once manual edits are complete move this file to the overrides folder.**

03_removed_duplicates.csv is saved to the steps folder. 
Medications and Supplements are extracted using local AI and reported with lowest AI 
confidence scores at the top fo the medications_manual_override.csv (saved to review).

Each AI recommended term is listed in the 'recommended_text' next to the full 'raw_text'
entry. 

##### Before running ensure:
-Download and install from: https://ollama.com/download'
-'med_sup_dict.csv' is in the ref folder


```{python}
#| label: 03 Remove duplicates and Review Meds

cleaner.step_03_removed_duplicates_and_review_meds()

```

##### Shared Birthdates
If any duplicates are clearly different people, the manual override must mark the flag_shared_birthdate column for the early submission. If the birthdates are not shared this must remain empty.

Flagging a row makes that specific row stay in the final dataset no mater what.

*e.g.*   all rows will be kept
id 1       bday 1/2/10       flag X

id 3       bday 1/2/10       flag X

id 4       bday 1/2/10       flag X


*e.g.*   the id 1 and id 4 will kept
id 1       bday 1/2/10       flag X

id 3       bday 1/2/10       flag

id 4       bday 1/2/10       flag


*e.g.*  id 3 and id 4 will kept
id 1       bday 1/2/10       flag

id 3       bday 1/2/10       flag

id 4       bday 1/2/10       flag X




# Clean and Review Medication and Supplement text reponses
Again, manually review and edit medications_manual_override.csv as needed, leaving
explanations in the overrides column of the manual_overrides file and general comments
in the txt.

Change the 'recommended_term' as needed (along with the 'from_column' if it was submitted
in the wrong column e.g. a supplement in seizure medication) 

The 'add_to_ref' column tracks 'MISSING IN REF' medicatons and supplements. 
If the 'recommended_term' is an alias add it to the alias column of the med_sup_dict.csv.
If it's a new medication or supplement, insert a new row with the 'functional_class' 
(if applicable), 'Generic Name', and any aliases/abbr. 
(All other columns are optional for your reference, and not included in the code.)


**Once manual edits are complete move 'medications_manual_override' to overrides folder.**


# 04 Inputs and categorizes medication overrides, and output medications for review







        to be continued...

```


# and Seizure Overrides. # Standardize by age
## Pre-Format Genetic text and p. and c. Col Review

??? Check entries accross multiple survey responses as needed Outputs p. and c. columns
from multiple surveys for each participant with conflicting inputs
creates new columns for both condensed and interpretable notation versions


Create Manual overrides file...

# Final Format Genetic text, Speculative ION channel Function, and Phenotype

'function?\_eric' and 'phenotype?\_eric' columns are based on Eric's AI generated spreadsheet based on a variety of Sources papers ('function?\_eric_ref')



# Speculative ION channel Function based on Participant Responses

I'm not sure if this is possible to compute for IS. You will probably need some adjustments
if you use this function.
'by_symp' is computed based on:
-seizure onset -seizure history -non-absense seizures -DQ (developmental quotient)\<50
'by_med' is computed based on:
-by current medication type -(and/or med history)
-seizure type (separate out myoclonic lof as separate category)
